name: intra_session_race
description: Triggers the intra-session race between handleEnd and handleData in the module's WorkersQueue. With small data_size and many concurrent agents, the End is dispatched by one worker before other workers finish observing all DataValues of the same session in GapSet → handleEnd sends ReqRet even though TCP delivered everything. This is NOT a queue drop; the DataValues are in RocksDB. It is a consequence of worker parallelism sharing m_workersQueue (std::thread::hardware_concurrency()).
notes: indexer_error and inventory_sync_error stay at 0 (not an indexer problem).
Diagnostic hint: compare with scenarios/baseline.json (data_size=50, agents=10) — the race is reduced or disappears with longer sessions or less parallelism.
Plausible mitigations (NOT included, these are production changes):
- Process messages from the same session on the same worker (sharding by sessionId).
- Wait for the session to drain before evaluating handleEnd.
- Reduce worker count for short sessions.
